title: Add post-deployment Keystone API health check
description:
# GitHub Issue #183: Add post-deployment Keystone API health verification

**Category**: feature | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: After reconcileDeployment confirms all replicas are available, add a health check sub-reconciler that performs an HTTP GET to the Keystone /v3 endpoint from within the reconciler. This verifies that the Keystone API is actually serving requests, not just that pods are running. Set a KeystoneAPIReady condition based on the probe result.

**Motivation**: A Deployment can report all replicas as ready while uWSGI serves 500s due to misconfigured keystone.conf, database connectivity issues, or other runtime failures. Deployment readiness only confirms the container started and passed its readiness probe — it does not verify end-to-end API functionality. This is a gap between infrastructure readiness and service readiness.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] New sub-reconciler or post-check performs HTTP GET to Keystone /v3 endpoint
- [ ] KeystoneAPIReady condition reflects actual API availability
- [ ] Configurable timeout and retry for the health check
- [ ] Reconciler handles cases where Service is not yet created or endpoints are not ready
- [ ] Unit test with mock HTTP server validates health check logic

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/183

stories.title: Operator verifies Keystone API is serving requests after deployment
stories.want: the reconciler to perform an HTTP GET to the Keystone /v3 endpoint after the Deployment reports ready
stories.so_that: I can trust that KeystoneAPIReady=True means the API is actually responding, not just that pods passed their readiness probe
criteria: After DeploymentReady=True, reconciler performs HTTP GET to http://{name}-api.{namespace}.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3
HTTP response status 200-299 sets KeystoneAPIReady=True with Reason 'APIHealthy'
HTTP response status >= 400 sets KeystoneAPIReady=False with Reason 'APIUnhealthy' and includes the HTTP status code in the message
KeystoneAPIReady condition includes ObservedGeneration matching keystone.Generation

stories.title: Operator requeues when health check fails transiently
stories.want: the reconciler to retry the health check on transient failures instead of permanently marking the API as unhealthy
stories.so_that: brief startup hiccups don't prevent the reconciler from eventually reaching Ready=True
criteria: When HTTP GET returns a non-2xx status, reconciler returns RequeueAfter with the health check polling interval
When HTTP GET times out (context deadline exceeded), reconciler sets KeystoneAPIReady=False with Reason 'HealthCheckTimeout' and requeues
When HTTP GET fails with connection refused, reconciler sets KeystoneAPIReady=False with Reason 'ConnectionFailed' and requeues
Requeue interval is defined as a constant in requeue_intervals.go

stories.title: Operator handles missing Service gracefully during health check
stories.want: the reconciler to handle cases where the Kubernetes Service or Endpoints are not yet resolvable
stories.so_that: the reconciler does not crash or return hard errors when DNS resolution fails for the in-cluster service
criteria: DNS resolution failure (Service not yet created) sets KeystoneAPIReady=False with Reason 'EndpointNotReady' and requeues
No panic or unrecoverable error is returned for DNS/connectivity failures
The health check uses the endpoint URL from keystone.Status.Endpoint which is set by reconcileDeployment

stories.title: Operator uses configurable timeout for health check HTTP requests
stories.want: the health check HTTP request to use a bounded timeout
stories.so_that: a hanging Keystone API does not block the reconciler indefinitely
criteria: HTTP client uses a context-derived timeout of 10 seconds for the health check request
Timeout is defined as a constant in requeue_intervals.go alongside other interval constants
If the request exceeds the timeout, KeystoneAPIReady=False is set with Reason 'HealthCheckTimeout'

stories.title: KeystoneAPIReady participates in aggregate Ready condition
stories.want: the KeystoneAPIReady condition to be included in the aggregate Ready evaluation
stories.so_that: Ready=True is only set when the API is verified healthy end-to-end, not just infrastructure-ready
criteria: KeystoneAPIReady is added to the subConditionTypes slice in keystone_controller.go
Ready=True requires KeystoneAPIReady=True in addition to all existing sub-conditions
Ready=False with Reason 'NotAllReady' when KeystoneAPIReady=False and all other conditions are True

stories.title: Health check sub-reconciler is testable with mock HTTP server
stories.want: the health check logic to be unit-testable with an injectable HTTP client interface
stories.so_that: tests can simulate healthy, unhealthy, timeout, and connection-refused scenarios without real network calls
criteria: Health check accepts an HTTPDoer interface (with Do method) for HTTP requests
KeystoneReconciler struct gains an HTTPClient field of type HTTPDoer
Unit tests use httptest.NewServer to simulate various response scenarios
Production code uses http.DefaultClient or a configured *http.Client

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL perform an HTTP GET to the Keystone /v3 endpoint after DeploymentReady=True and set KeystoneAPIReady based on the response
requirements.rationale: A Deployment reporting all replicas ready does not guarantee the Keystone API is serving valid responses — uWSGI may return 500s due to config, DB, or runtime errors

scenarios.name: Healthy API sets condition True
scenarios.when: DeploymentReady=True and HTTP GET to /v3 returns status 200
scenarios.then: KeystoneAPIReady condition is set to True with Reason 'APIHealthy'
and_then: Message includes 'Keystone API is responding at {endpoint}'
Reconciler proceeds to the next sub-reconciler without requeue

scenarios.name: Unhealthy API sets condition False and requeues
scenarios.when: DeploymentReady=True and HTTP GET to /v3 returns status 500
scenarios.then: KeystoneAPIReady condition is set to False with Reason 'APIUnhealthy'
and_then: Message includes the HTTP status code (e.g., 'HTTP 500')
Reconciler returns RequeueAfter with health check interval

scenarios.name: Non-success status codes treated as unhealthy
scenarios.when: DeploymentReady=True and HTTP GET to /v3 returns status 503 Service Unavailable
scenarios.then: KeystoneAPIReady condition is set to False with Reason 'APIUnhealthy'
and_then: Response body is closed to prevent resource leaks

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL use a bounded timeout for health check HTTP requests to prevent blocking the reconcile loop
requirements.rationale: A hanging Keystone API (e.g., stuck on DB connection) must not block the entire reconciler indefinitely

scenarios.name: Request completes within timeout
scenarios.when: HTTP GET to /v3 responds within the 10-second timeout
scenarios.then: The response is processed normally (success or failure path)

scenarios.name: Request exceeds timeout
scenarios.when: HTTP GET to /v3 does not respond within 10 seconds
scenarios.then: KeystoneAPIReady is set to False with Reason 'HealthCheckTimeout'
and_then: Reconciler returns RequeueAfter with health check interval

scenarios.name: Timeout constant is defined alongside other requeue intervals
scenarios.when: The health check timeout constant is referenced
scenarios.then: It is defined in requeue_intervals.go as HealthCheckTimeout = 10 * time.Second

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL handle connection-level failures (DNS, TCP) gracefully without returning hard errors
requirements.rationale: The Service may not yet have Endpoints, DNS may not resolve, or the pod may have just started — these are transient conditions, not reconciler bugs

scenarios.name: Connection refused (no endpoints)
scenarios.when: HTTP GET fails with 'connection refused' (Service has no ready endpoints)
scenarios.then: KeystoneAPIReady is set to False with Reason 'ConnectionFailed'

scenarios.name: DNS resolution failure
scenarios.when: HTTP GET fails because the Service DNS name cannot be resolved
scenarios.then: KeystoneAPIReady is set to False with Reason 'EndpointNotReady'

scenarios.name: Generic network error
scenarios.when: HTTP GET fails with an unexpected network error
scenarios.then: KeystoneAPIReady is set to False with Reason 'HealthCheckFailed'

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL use keystone.Status.Endpoint as the health check target URL
requirements.rationale: Status.Endpoint is set by reconcileDeployment and represents the canonical in-cluster URL

scenarios.name: Endpoint set by prior reconciler
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment has set keystone.Status.Endpoint to a valid URL
scenarios.then: The health check performs HTTP GET to that exact URL

scenarios.name: Endpoint is empty
scenarios.when: keystone.Status.Endpoint is empty when reconcileHealthCheck is called
scenarios.then: KeystoneAPIReady is set to False with Reason 'EndpointNotReady'

requirements.description: The KeystoneAPIReady condition SHALL participate in the aggregate Ready condition evaluation
requirements.rationale: Ready=True must reflect end-to-end service readiness including actual API health

scenarios.name: All conditions True including KeystoneAPIReady
scenarios.when: All sub-conditions including KeystoneAPIReady are True
scenarios.then: Ready is set to True with Reason 'AllReady'

scenarios.name: KeystoneAPIReady False blocks overall readiness
scenarios.when: All sub-conditions are True except KeystoneAPIReady
scenarios.then: Ready is set to False with Reason 'NotAllReady'

scenarios.name: subConditionTypes includes KeystoneAPIReady
scenarios.when: subConditionTypes is inspected
scenarios.then: It contains 'KeystoneAPIReady' between 'DeploymentReady' and 'HPAReady'

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL use an injectable HTTP client interface for testability
requirements.rationale: Unit tests must simulate HTTP responses deterministically without real network calls

scenarios.name: Nil HTTPClient falls back to http.DefaultClient
scenarios.when: KeystoneReconciler.HTTPClient is nil
scenarios.then: http.DefaultClient is used for the health check

scenarios.name: Injected HTTPClient is used
scenarios.when: KeystoneReconciler.HTTPClient is set to a mock
scenarios.then: The mock's Do method is called
and_then: http.Client satisfies the interface without adapter code

requirements.description: The health check sub-reconciler SHALL execute after reconcileDeployment and before reconcileHPA in the reconciliation chain
requirements.rationale: Health check depends on DeploymentReady=True and Service existence; must run before downstream sub-reconcilers that assume a healthy API

scenarios.name: Runs after successful deployment reconciliation
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment returns (ctrl.Result{}, nil)
scenarios.then: reconcileHealthCheck is called next
and_then: If it requeues, reconcileHPA and later sub-reconcilers are skipped

scenarios.name: Skipped when deployment not ready
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment returns (ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: 10s}, nil)
scenarios.then: reconcileHealthCheck is NOT called

requirements.description: The health check SHALL close HTTP response bodies to prevent resource leaks
requirements.rationale: Unclosed response bodies in a long-running controller cause goroutine and connection pool leaks

scenarios.name: Success response body closed
scenarios.when: HTTP GET returns 200
scenarios.then: resp.Body.Close() is called via defer

scenarios.name: Error response body closed
scenarios.when: HTTP GET returns 500
scenarios.then: resp.Body.Close() is called via defer

tasks.title: Add HTTPDoer interface and HTTPClient field to KeystoneReconciler (REQ-006)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_healthcheck.go, define the HTTPDoer interface with a single Do(*http.Request) (*http.Response, error) method. Add an HTTPClient HTTPDoer field to the KeystoneReconciler struct in keystone_controller.go. Add a helper method httpClient() that returns HTTPClient if non-nil, else http.DefaultClient. Unit test in reconcile_healthcheck_test.go: verify httpClient() returns the injected client when set and http.DefaultClient when nil.

tasks.title: Add health check constants to requeue_intervals.go (REQ-002)
tasks.description: Add RequeueHealthCheck = 10 * time.Second and HealthCheckTimeout = 10 * time.Second to requeue_intervals.go with doc comments following the existing pattern. RequeueHealthCheck is the interval for requeuing on health check failure. HealthCheckTimeout is the bounded timeout for the HTTP request to prevent blocking the reconcile loop.

tasks.title: Add 'KeystoneAPIReady' to subConditionTypes (REQ-005)
tasks.description: Add 'KeystoneAPIReady' to the subConditionTypes slice in keystone_controller.go, positioned after 'DeploymentReady' and before 'HPAReady'. Unit test in keystone_controller_test.go: verify aggregateReady returns false when KeystoneAPIReady is missing from conditions, and true when all conditions including KeystoneAPIReady are True.

tasks.title: Implement reconcileHealthCheck happy path — HTTP 2xx sets True, non-2xx sets False (REQ-001, REQ-004, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Create operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_healthcheck.go with the reconcileHealthCheck method. The method: (1) checks keystone.Status.Endpoint is non-empty, returning False/EndpointNotReady if empty; (2) creates an HTTP GET request with a context timeout of HealthCheckTimeout; (3) calls httpClient().Do(req); (4) if err != nil, classifies the error and sets condition; (5) if resp received, defers resp.Body.Close(), checks status 200-299 for True/APIHealthy, else False/APIUnhealthy with status code in message; (6) returns ctrl.Result{} on success or ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: RequeueHealthCheck} on failure. Unit tests using httptest.NewServer: test healthy 200, unhealthy 500, unhealthy 503, and verify ObservedGeneration is set.

tasks.title: Implement reconcileHealthCheck — empty endpoint handling with test (REQ-004)
tasks.description: In reconcile_healthcheck.go, ensure the empty endpoint check returns KeystoneAPIReady=False with Reason 'EndpointNotReady' and Message 'endpoint not yet configured'. Unit test in reconcile_healthcheck_test.go: create a Keystone CR with empty Status.Endpoint, call reconcileHealthCheck, verify condition is False/EndpointNotReady and result.RequeueAfter equals RequeueHealthCheck.

tasks.title: Implement reconcileHealthCheck — timeout error classification with test (REQ-002, REQ-003)
tasks.description: In reconcile_healthcheck.go, add error classification logic: (1) context.DeadlineExceeded or os.ErrDeadlineExceeded → Reason 'HealthCheckTimeout'; (2) *net.DNSError → Reason 'EndpointNotReady'; (3) connection refused (string match on 'connection refused') → Reason 'ConnectionFailed'; (4) fallback → Reason 'HealthCheckFailed'. All network errors set KeystoneAPIReady=False and return RequeueAfter (not error). Unit tests with mock HTTPDoer returning each error type.

tasks.title: Implement reconcileHealthCheck — connection refused and DNS error tests (REQ-003)
tasks.description: Unit tests in reconcile_healthcheck_test.go: (1) connection refused — mock HTTPDoer returns a *url.Error wrapping 'connection refused' — verify condition False/ConnectionFailed and RequeueAfter; (2) DNS failure — mock HTTPDoer returns a *net.DNSError — verify condition False/EndpointNotReady; (3) generic error — mock returns plain error — verify condition False/HealthCheckFailed. All tests verify ObservedGeneration matches keystone.Generation.

tasks.title: Wire reconcileHealthCheck into Reconcile() chain after reconcileDeployment (REQ-007)
tasks.description: In keystone_controller.go Reconcile(), add the reconcileHealthCheck call after reconcileDeployment and before reconcileHPA, following the same pattern: if result, err := r.reconcileHealthCheck(ctx, &keystone); !result.IsZero() || err != nil { return r.updateStatus(...) }. Unit test in keystone_controller_test.go: verify reconcileHealthCheck requeue prevents HPA and later sub-reconcilers from running.

tasks.title: Add response body close verification test and health check chain ordering test (REQ-007, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Unit tests: (1) verify HTTP response body is closed after processing for both 200 and 500 responses by using a custom ReadCloser that tracks Close() calls; (2) verify the full reconcile chain calls reconcileHealthCheck when DeploymentReady passes and skips it when deployment is not ready.

tasks.title: Update keystone-reconciler.md with KeystoneAPIReady condition and reconcileHealthCheck sub-reconciler (REQ-001, REQ-005)
tasks.description: Update docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md: (1) add KeystoneAPIReady to the Sub-Condition Types table with Set By 'reconcileHealthCheck' and Description 'Keystone /v3 endpoint responding with HTTP 2xx'; (2) add a reconcileHealthCheck section following the existing sub-reconciler documentation pattern (File, Signature, Purpose, Condition Contract table with all Reason/Message/RequeueAfter values); (3) update the Reconciliation Flow ASCII diagram to include the health check step between reconcileDeployment and reconcileHPA. Update docs/reference/keystone-crd.md: add KeystoneAPIReady to the conditions example.

test_specifications.story: Operator verifies Keystone API is serving requests after deployment
test_specifications.expected: HTTP 200 response sets KeystoneAPIReady=True with Reason 'APIHealthy' and no requeue [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator verifies Keystone API is serving requests after deployment
test_specifications.expected: HTTP 500 response sets KeystoneAPIReady=False with Reason 'APIUnhealthy', message includes '500', and requeues [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator verifies Keystone API is serving requests after deployment
test_specifications.expected: HTTP 503 response sets KeystoneAPIReady=False with Reason 'APIUnhealthy' and requeues [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator verifies Keystone API is serving requests after deployment
test_specifications.expected: KeystoneAPIReady condition ObservedGeneration matches keystone.Generation for both True and False states [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator requeues when health check fails transiently
test_specifications.expected: Context deadline exceeded error sets KeystoneAPIReady=False with Reason 'HealthCheckTimeout' and requeues [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator uses configurable timeout for health check HTTP requests
test_specifications.expected: HealthCheckTimeout constant equals 10 * time.Second and is defined in requeue_intervals.go [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator requeues when health check fails transiently
test_specifications.expected: Connection refused error sets KeystoneAPIReady=False with Reason 'ConnectionFailed' and requeues [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator handles missing Service gracefully during health check
test_specifications.expected: DNS resolution failure sets KeystoneAPIReady=False with Reason 'EndpointNotReady' and requeues [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator handles missing Service gracefully during health check
test_specifications.expected: Generic network error sets KeystoneAPIReady=False with Reason 'HealthCheckFailed' and requeues [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator handles missing Service gracefully during health check
test_specifications.expected: Empty keystone.Status.Endpoint sets KeystoneAPIReady=False with Reason 'EndpointNotReady' and requeues [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator verifies Keystone API is serving requests after deployment
test_specifications.expected: Health check HTTP request targets exactly keystone.Status.Endpoint URL [unit]

test_specifications.story: KeystoneAPIReady participates in aggregate Ready condition
test_specifications.expected: subConditionTypes slice contains 'KeystoneAPIReady' after 'DeploymentReady' and before 'HPAReady' [unit]

test_specifications.story: KeystoneAPIReady participates in aggregate Ready condition
test_specifications.expected: aggregateReady returns false when KeystoneAPIReady is not set even if all other conditions are True [unit]

test_specifications.story: Health check sub-reconciler is testable with mock HTTP server
test_specifications.expected: httpClient() returns the injected HTTPDoer when HTTPClient is non-nil [unit]

test_specifications.story: Health check sub-reconciler is testable with mock HTTP server
test_specifications.expected: httpClient() returns http.DefaultClient when HTTPClient is nil [unit]

test_specifications.story: KeystoneAPIReady participates in aggregate Ready condition
test_specifications.expected: Full Reconcile() calls reconcileHealthCheck after reconcileDeployment succeeds, and health check requeue prevents HPA from running [unit]

test_specifications.story: KeystoneAPIReady participates in aggregate Ready condition
test_specifications.expected: When reconcileDeployment requeues, reconcileHealthCheck is NOT called and KeystoneAPIReady condition is not set [unit]

test_specifications.story: Health check sub-reconciler is testable with mock HTTP server
test_specifications.expected: HTTP response body is closed after processing a 200 response, verified via a custom ReadCloser that tracks Close() calls [unit]

test_specifications.story: Health check sub-reconciler is testable with mock HTTP server
test_specifications.expected: HTTP response body is closed after processing a 500 response, verified via a custom ReadCloser that tracks Close() calls [unit]

review_criteria: All 8 SHALL requirements (REQ-001 to REQ-008) have corresponding passing unit tests

reconcileHealthCheck follows the existing sub-reconciler pattern: same signature (ctx, *Keystone) → (ctrl.Result, error), same condition-setting pattern using conditions.SetCondition with ObservedGeneration
HTTPDoer interface is minimal (single Do method) and http.Client satisfies it without adapter code
All network errors (timeout, DNS, connection refused, generic) result in condition False + RequeueAfter, never in returned errors that would trigger controller-runtime exponential backoff
HTTP response bodies are closed via defer in all code paths that receive a non-nil response
subConditionTypes ordering matches the reconciliation chain order (DeploymentReady → KeystoneAPIReady → HPAReady)
Constants (RequeueHealthCheck, HealthCheckTimeout) are co-located with other interval constants in requeue_intervals.go
Documentation in docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md includes the new condition type, sub-reconciler section, and updated ASCII flow diagram
go vet and golangci-lint pass with no new warnings
implementation_notes:
## Architecture

The health check is implemented as a new sub-reconciler `reconcileHealthCheck` following the exact pattern of existing sub-reconcilers (reconcileSecrets, reconcileDeployment, etc.). It slots into the reconciliation chain between `reconcileDeployment` and `reconcileHPA`.

## Data Flow

```
reconcileDeployment (sets Status.Endpoint, DeploymentReady=True)
        │
        ▼
reconcileHealthCheck
  1. Read keystone.Status.Endpoint (set by prior step)
  2. Create HTTP GET request with HealthCheckTimeout context
  3. Call httpClient().Do(req)
  4. Classify result → set KeystoneAPIReady condition
  5. Return ctrl.Result{} or ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: RequeueHealthCheck}
        │
        ▼
reconcileHPA (only runs if health check passes)
```

## Key Design Decisions

1. **HTTPDoer interface over concrete http.Client**: Enables unit testing with mock HTTP servers (httptest.NewServer) and mock error injection. The interface has a single method matching http.Client.Do, so no adapter is needed.

2. **Network errors → condition False + requeue, not returned errors**: Connection refused, DNS failures, and timeouts are expected transient states during deployment convergence. Returning them as errors would trigger controller-runtime's exponential backoff, delaying recovery. Instead, we set a descriptive condition and requeue at a fixed interval.

3. **Using Status.Endpoint**: The endpoint URL is already computed by reconcileDeployment (http://{name}-api.{namespace}.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3). Reusing it avoids URL construction duplication and ensures the health check targets the same endpoint that consumers will use.

4. **10-second timeout**: Matches the Deployment polling interval (RequeueDeploymentPolling = 10s). Long enough for a cold start but short enough to not block the reconcile loop.

## Files Modified

- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_healthcheck.go` (NEW) — sub-reconciler implementation + HTTPDoer interface
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_healthcheck_test.go` (NEW) — unit tests
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go` — add HTTPClient field, wire reconcileHealthCheck into chain, add to subConditionTypes
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/requeue_intervals.go` — add RequeueHealthCheck and HealthCheckTimeout constants
- `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md` — add condition type and sub-reconciler documentation
- `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md` — add KeystoneAPIReady to conditions example

## Patterns to Follow

- Sub-reconciler pattern: see reconcile_secrets.go (condition setting, requeue, error handling)
- Test pattern: see reconcile_deployment_test.go (scheme setup, fake client, Gomega assertions)
- Condition pattern: see internal/common/conditions/conditions.go (SetCondition with metav1.Condition)
- Requeue constants: see requeue_intervals.go (centralized, documented)

## Pitfalls

- **Do not return HTTP errors as reconcile errors**: This would trigger exponential backoff instead of fixed-interval requeue
- **Always close response bodies**: Even on non-2xx responses, Go net/http returns a non-nil Body
- **Handle nil HTTPClient in production**: The SetupWithManager path does not set HTTPClient, so httpClient() must fall back to http.DefaultClient
